content.text:
Machine Learning
Semester:
2  |
Type:
UEM2  |
VHS:
63h  |
Weekly hours:
C: 1h30 / TD: 1h30 / TP: 1h30  |
Personal work VHS:
42h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
This module covers an introduction to the field, followed by an overview of supervised and unsupervised learning techniques, then an exploration of more advanced topics such as deep learning and natural language processing. It also addresses essential mathematical foundations (linear algebra, statistics), Python programming and practical aspects such as data preprocessing, feature engineering, model selection, training, evaluation and deployment.
Recommended prior knowledge:
Mathematics: linear algebra, probability, statistics, etc.
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction and Fundamentals of Machine Learning
Machine learning: definition and application domains
Types of learning (supervised, unsupervised, semi-supervised, reinforcement, autonomous)
Mathematical foundations: linear algebra, statistics and probability, random variables and distributions, Bayesian thinking
Machine learning workflow: stages of an ML project (from data collection to deployment)
Supervised Learning
Regression: linear, polynomial, etc.
Classification: logistic regression, support vector machines (SVM), decision trees and ensemble methods (random forests, gradient boosting)
Model evaluation: metrics for regression and classification (RMSE, accuracy, precision, recall, F1 score)
Unsupervised Learning
Clustering: K-means, hierarchical clustering, density-based (DBSCAN, OPTICS)
Evaluation metrics: silhouette score, Davies-Bouldin index, adjusted Rand index, mutual information
Dimensionality reduction: PCA, t-SNE, LDA, ICA, UMAP, factor analysis
Anomaly detection: outlier identification
Association Rule Learning: Apriori, Eclat, FP-Growth algorithms
Deep Learning
Introduction to neural networks: basic architecture, operating principles
Deep learning architectures: CNN, RNN
Training deep learning models: TensorFlow, PyTorch
Advanced Topics
Natural Language Processing (NLP)
Time series analysis
Recommendation systems
Reinforcement learning
Assessment
In-person semester exam (60%)
Continuous assessment (CC) (40%): continuous tests, personal work
References
Andreas Müller and Sarah Guido (2016).
Introduction to Machine Learning with Python
. O'Reilly.
Chapelle, O., Scholkopf, B. et Zien, A. (2009). Semi-Supervised Learning.
IEEE Transactions on Neural Networks
, 20(3), 542–542.
Batta, M. (2018). Machine Learning Algorithms: A Review. ResearchGate.
